steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B (-9)
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (-9):
From the second equation $$-3x+y=3$$ we solve for $$y=3+3x$$.
Substitute $$x=-9$$: $$y=3+3(-9)=-24$$.
Check the first equation $$x^{2}+y=111$$: $$(-9)^{2}+(-24)=81-24=57\neq111$$.
Thus choice B does not satisfy the system.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice A (-12)
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice A (-12):
From $$-3x+y=3$$ we get $$y=3+3x$$.
With $$x=-12$$: $$y=3+3(-12)=-33$$.
Check $$x^{2}+y=111$$: $$(-12)^{2}+(-33)=144-33=111$$.
Both equations are satisfied.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
